input: Hello doctor,I am a 46 year old male. I have a desk job where I need to be sitting for eight hours continuously at the computer. I am suffering from these problems since 27 years. When I was 19, I did heavy exercises once and suddenly the heartbeat increased to approximately 180 times per minute. It came back to the normal rate in a minute. But, from that day I have been having heart palpitations. I stopped all the exercises. I tested ECG and 2D. Both the tests were normal. The doctor said my heart is \almost\ normal. But another doctor said that it may be a valve problem. He said this just without testings. But my experiences are \heart palpitations\ and I feel some \weight\ in the heart continuously for four to five months.Then I felt normal and tried doing the exercise again. I feel the same experience. So, again I stopped exercising. So many times these repeated. Three months back, I experienced the symptoms. I felt giddy too. So, I feel very afraid. I immediately consulted the cardiologist. But ECG, 2D, and treadmill (this is first time tested) came normal. I did not test the angiogram. Now I have breathing difficulty, dizziness, and uneven heartbeats when I lie on my side. I quit exercises completely. I am only walking. I do not understand what is going on. Am I straining myself during exercise? Or is it a serious problem? Please help me.
answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. You have done some tests already related to the heart and found it to be normal. I suggest you do free T4, T3, TSH, Holter monitoring, and Hb %. Based on the reports, the next course of action could be taken. I think you need not worry. Try to control your stress. Do breathing exercise for five minutes two times daily and Yoga for half an hour. Listen to music, spend quality times with friends and family. For more information consult a cardiologist online
answer_chatgpt: Hello! It sounds like you are experiencing some concerning symptoms related to your heart. It is important that you continue to follow up with your cardiologist and discuss all of your symptoms with them. While it is possible that your palpitations and discomfort are related to a valve problem, it is difficult to say without further investigation. There are many different conditions that can cause these symptoms, including arrhythmias, cardiomyopathy, and coronary artery disease. Your cardiologist may recommend further testing, such as a Holter monitor or event recorder, to monitor your heart rhythm over a longer period of time. They may also consider performing additional imaging studies, such as an echocardiogram or cardiac MRI, to evaluate your heart's structure and function. In the meantime, it is important that you continue to take care of yourself by staying physically active (within your limits), eating a healthy diet, and avoiding tobacco and excessive alcohol use. Stress management techniques such as relaxation exercises, yoga, or meditation may also be beneficial. Please do not hesitate to seek medical attention if you experience any new or worsening symptoms.
answer_chatdoctor: Hi, Thanks for writing in. I can understand your concern and would try to explain as much as possible. Yes, it is possible that you might be feeling more palpitation if you overdo any physical activity or stressful situation. This happens due to the increase in the blood flow to the brain which causes the heart to beat faster. But, it is essential to get yourself evaluated by a cardiologist who will examine you and also perform certain investigations like an echocardiography and an electrophysiology study to look into the cause of these symptoms. You should also discuss with him about starting beta blockers which will help reduce the frequency of the palpitations. In case they persist, he may also suggest a pacemaker implantation. Hope this helps.

input: Hi doctor,I had wart-like growth on my foreskin. I consulted a doctor, who asked me to get a circumcision done. So I got the circumcision done. The biopsy result showed that they were viral warts (condyloma acuminatum), and the doctor gave me tablet Valtoval for a month. After going through the internet, I found out that viral warts are caused by HPV, so I stopped taking Valtoval, as it is used for herpes and not HPV. But now, I got a small wart-like lesion on my penis. I feel bad and frustrated, and I do not know what I need to do. Please help me. What do I need to do? I am going to get married next month. I am waiting for your response. Thanks.
answer_icliniq: Hello. I read your query and understand your concerns. You are right in saying that genital warts are caused by HPV (human papillomavirus), and even the biopsy of the excised skin confirmed the same. Although there are some reports that Valtoval (Valacyclovir) has some efficacy in HPV, it is largely inconclusive. Having said this, I think you need to opt for Imiquimod or Podophyllin as next mode of treatment. The former enhances your immune system, which helps in fighting from genital warts. In my own practice, I prefer this mode of treatment and find good results. I request you to discuss the use of Imiquimod with your doctor. At the same time, avoid unprotected sex with your potential partner now or in recent future, as it may transmit the disease to your partner. I must assure you that with Imiquimod, there is a good chance of remission of genital warts. I hope this helps you. Feel free to write back if you have more questions. Thanks and regards. For more information consult a sexologist online
answer_chatgpt:
I am sorry to hear about your situation. It is true that viral warts, also known as condyloma acuminatum, are caused by the human papillomavirus (HPV). The best course of action is to consult a dermatologist or urologist who can evaluate the area and provide appropriate treatment.

It is important to complete the course of medication as prescribed by your doctor, as stopping it prematurely may not effectively treat the virus. While there is no cure for HPV, there are various treatment options available to help manage the symptoms, including cryotherapy (freezing the lesions), laser treatment, topical creams or gels, and surgical removal. Your healthcare provider will assess the size, number, and location of the lesions to determine the best treatment approach.

It is essential to disclose your medical history, including any sexually transmitted infections, to your partner before engaging in sexual activity. HPV is a sexually transmitted infection that can be passed on through sexual contact, even if there are no visible symptoms.

For individuals who are sexually active, it is recommended to get regular check-ups with a healthcare provider, practice safe sex by using condoms, and limit the number of sexual partners to reduce the risk of contracting or spreading sexually transmitted infections.

I hope this helps, and I wish you the best of luck with your treatment and upcoming wedding.
answer_chatdoctor: 1. Viral warts can be treated with topical immunomodulator like diclofenac sodium gel or Aldara cream over affected area.2. You may also use a topical antiviral like podophyllotoxin cream.3. You may try an oral antiviral such as acyclovir if there is no improvement in few days.4. Circumcision will not prevent you from getting warts and it needs to be done only if warts are painful or causing discomfort while urination.5. You should avoid sexual intercourse until the warts completely heals.6. You may also apply a condom during sex to reduce chance of spreading the warts to your partner. Hope this helps

input: Hi doctor,I am a 53 year old female. My periods have been delayed for two weeks. Till now they are normally regular. Could it be the early onset of menopause or they will eventually come?
answer_icliniq: Hello. If you have been regular with your menses these years and this is the first episode at 53 years of age when the menses have been delayed by two weeks, the causes can be a possible initiation of perimenopausal stage where menses are often delayed for a month or two and slowly tend to stop in next one to two years. Perimenopausal stage can be verified with the hormonal FSH (follicle stimulating hormone) and LH (Luteinizing hormone) levels which if high shall indicate an onset of perimenopausal stage. However, it is suggested that pregnancy should anyways be ruled out and if negative then you can wait naturally for your menses to resume. Hence, try maintaining a menstrual calendar and keep a mark for your dates, duration of flow and quantity to evaluate the possible onset of a perimenopausal state.

input: Hello doctor,I have a clear bubble in the sclera of my eye in the corner close to the nose. This has not gotten any bigger and sometimes it appears smaller. It does not hurt. I have had it for years. My eyes dry a lot and I have an issue with dry eyes and allergies. Could this be serious? Sometimes my eye in the corner is sticky as well. Please help.
answer_icliniq: Hello. This appears to be a conjunctival cyst. It is not harmful. This can be caused by a mild foreign body sensation. I suggest you start Fluorometholone plus Tobramycin eye ChatDoctor.  You need to then stop this eye
answer_chatgpt:
Hello,

Based on your description, it's likely that the clear bubble in the corner of your eye is a conjunctival cyst. Conjunctival cysts are small, fluid-filled sacs that form on the clear tissue that covers the white part of the eye (conjunctiva). Conjunctival cysts are generally harmless and don't require treatment, but they can be bothersome, especially if they are large or interfere with vision. 

Given that you've had this for years and it hasn't grown or become painful, it's unlikely to be serious. The stickiness in the corner of your eye could be due to dryness or allergies. You may consider using artificial tears to help lubricate your eyes and relieve dryness.

However, I recommend that you visit an eye doctor to get a proper diagnosis and to rule out any serious conditions. The eye doctor may recommend observation or removal of the cyst if it's causing discomfort. Additionally, the eye doctor can recommend a treatment plan for your dry eyes and allergies.

I hope this helps!

input: Hello doctor,I am 32 years old, and I am kind of facing the issue of fantasy in my head and mind deconcentration. Whenever I am busy or in social gathering most of the time I am fine. But when I am alone, walking alone and traveling alone, my mind goes into the fantasy. I kind of see this thing becoming common in people and while observing them I noticed that whenever I am in this situation my body is at rest while facial expression is changing according to what I am thinking. As I watched a lot of fantasies movies and seasons, my mind keeps stuck in these, thinking about different scenarios along with facial expression like smiling, anger, etc. It is kind of embarrassing when someone notices me. I would like to know what it is. People are saying it as daydreaming. My question is whether it is really daydreaming, or if not what it is? How can I overcome it?
answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. Yes, you are suffering from excessive imagination or day ChatDoctor.  But some people do it more which becomes a kind of habit. Sometimes, we also use this kind of defense and imagination in order to avoid day-to-day stress. It becomes a form of avoidance behavior. Distract yourself with some positive and productive strategies like focus on your career, practice your hobbies, make a day-to-day schedule, try to follow it, use some aversive stimulation to prevent yourself from going deep in imagination like pinching yourself hard on the hand.
answer_chatgpt:
Hello, 

Based on your description, it is possible that you are experiencing daydreaming or maladaptive daydreaming. Daydreaming is a natural and healthy activity, but when it becomes excessive and interferes with daily functioning, it is considered maladaptive daydreaming. Some common symptoms of maladaptive daydreaming include feeling absorbed in a fantasy world, difficulty controlling the daydreams, and experiencing distress or impairment as a result of the daydreaming. 

To overcome maladaptive daydreaming, there are several strategies that can be helpful. First, it may be helpful to develop a regular routine that keeps you busy and engaged throughout the day. This can help prevent periods of boredom or isolation that may trigger daydreaming. Additionally, it may be helpful to identify triggers or patterns that contribute to the daydreaming and work to avoid or manage them. Additionally, practicing mindfulness and focusing on the present moment may be helpful in reducing the frequency and intensity of daydreaming. 

If your symptoms persist or significantly impact your daily functioning, it may be beneficial to speak with a mental health professional for further evaluation and treatment recommendations.
answer_chatdoctor: 1)Firstly, you should understand that daydreaming does occur in healthy individuals but only during leisure hours and not otherwise. The frequency and intensity of daydreaming increases with age. In your case, it seems to be more than normal. So, your concern is valid. 2) Secondly, the way you have described your symptoms makes it clear that you are suffering from Obsessional Compulsive Disorder (OCD). OCD is characterized by recurrent obsessions and compulsions which distress the individual and interfere with his/her daily life. 3) You need to consult a psychiatrist for expert help. You may require an anti-depressant Chat Doctor.

input: Hello doctor,Do I need an anti-rabies vaccine, because my 2 and a half-month-old puppy nipped me? It is barely a bite but I cleaned it and no symptoms seem to show up, my puppy is not vaccinated yet because it is too young. I am 18 years old.
answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. If the bite was unprovoked and if it caused bleeding then you should get a course of anti-rabies vaccine plus a shot of tetanus immunoglobulin, to be on the safe side. Even though it is a pet, it is not immunized so the standard protocol is to get vaccinated if the above-mentioned factors are present. Also, wash the wound with soap and water thoroughly. If the skin was not broken and if the attack was provoked, then it is not necessary to get vaccinated. However, given the nil immunization status of your pet, you may want to be on the safe side and get the shots.
answer_chatgpt:
Dear, it is always recommended to get an anti-rabies vaccine if you have been bitten or scratched by an animal, especially if it is a stray or unvaccinated animal. Rabies is a serious viral disease that can be transmitted from animals to humans through their saliva. In dogs and cats, it can cause aggressive behavior, foaming at the mouth, and other neurological symptoms.

Since your puppy is still young and not yet vaccinated, there is a chance it may be carrying the rabies virus, although it is not very common for puppies to have rabies. However, to be on the safe side, it's best to have the wound examined by a doctor and follow their advice.

The doctor may recommend a course of anti-rabies vaccine, which usually involves a series of four or five injections given over a period of few weeks. This vaccine is very effective in preventing rabies infection, but it is crucial to begin treatment as soon as possible after exposure. Therefore, it's important not to delay seeking medical attention if you have been bitten or scratched by an animal.
answer_chatdoctor: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. As per your history it seems that you were bitten by dog. You have not mentioned whether it was a stray dog or known one. Rabies virus spread from saliva of infected animal through bite wound. In case of dog bite if animal is known to be rabies free then there is no need for anti rabies vaccination. But in case it is unknown then you should take anti rabies vaccination as soon as possible.

input: Hello doctor,I am a 36 year old, otherwise healthy (as far as I know) female.I was a smoker earlier. No history of heart disease.My mother has COPD, but has been a long time smoker. Two and a half weeks ago, I went on a hayride with my sons.I have a short stature. To get on and off the hayride, I literally had to pull myself up onto the trailer. (Imagine pulling yourself out of a swimming pool).The next morning, I woke up with chest soreness.I had pain in the upper left chest area, a few inches to the left of sternum.That was a radiating pain over the breast towards armpit.Pain worsened on bending over.Also, I could locate very tender spots when pressing on the area with fingers.I had no pain during regular breathing, but very deep breaths caused pain to worsen (from the left of sternum radiating across and to the left breast). Over the past two and a half weeks, pain has improved, but still not completely subsided.Most of the pain (while bending) is gone, but I still have tender areas across upper left chest when poking around with finger (trigger points?).I have some minor discomfort on taking deep breath, but greatly improved from what it was.And now pain is just in an area to the left of my left breast.Now, pain is mostly towards armpit area and may be slightly radiating to the upper, bottom side of arm.I do not have much pain as it was in the beginning, but more of soreness.It is not constant, it occurs mostly just when I move in a certain way or tense my chest.Also I am noticing upper left back discomfort, not really pain. Local FNP suspects strained muscle. No x-rays were done.I have no cough, no shortness of breath, no loss of appetite, no indigestion or heartburn.Otherwise I feel fine. My concern is that I still have some pain and discomfort after almost 3 weeks.Shouldn\t a muscle strain be healed by now?I am a bit concerned as it may be something more serious than a strained muscle, perhaps pleurisy or costochondritis?
answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. You are right, muscular pain will not last so long. Pleurisy: Chostochon ChatDoctor.  Start taking analgesic and hot fomentation 2-3 times a day for the painful region. Use a thoracic belt to restrict movements of the affected part.  Get a chest x-ray done immediately and revert back to a general practitioner online
answer_chatgpt:
Based on your description, it is possible that you strained a muscle in your chest or upper back during the hayride. However, given the tenderness in specific areas and the persistence of symptoms after 2.5 weeks, it is also reasonable to consider other potential causes of your chest and armpit pain.

Costochondritis, which is inflammation of the cartilage that connects your ribs to your sternum, can cause tenderness and pain in the chest, especially in the upper left area. It can be triggered by physical activity or injury, and typically improves with rest and anti-inflammatory medications.

Pleurisy, which is inflammation of the lining surrounding the lungs, can also cause chest pain that worsens with deep breaths. It is often accompanied by other symptoms such as cough, fever, and shortness of breath.

Given that your symptoms have persisted for almost 3 weeks, I recommend that you follow up with your healthcare provider for a thorough evaluation. They may consider imaging tests such as a chest X-ray or CT scan to assess for potential underlying causes. Treatment will depend on the underlying cause, but may include pain management, physical therapy, or antibiotics if an infection is suspected.

It is important to note that while it is possible that your symptoms are related to your prior smoking history or your family history of COPD, there is not enough information in your description to definitively establish a connection. Nonetheless, quitting smoking and minimizing exposure to secondhand smoke can significantly reduce the risk of developing respiratory and cardiovascular problems.

However, if they persist for more than 2 weeks, there is always a possibility that it is something else. This is especially true since you seem to have other associated symptoms such as pain which increases on deep breathing and localized pain. 2) In my opinion, you need to see a specialist who can examine you and decide whether you need further tests. The most common test would be an x-ray of your chest. Hope this helps!

input: Hello doctor,My husband is away from the past one and half years due to his work. I visited him for 15 days. I had my normal periods there on the 31st of last month. 1. Is it safe? Or unsafe?

The keto diet can be safe for most healthy individuals if done correctly and under the guidance of a healthcare professional. However, people with certain medical conditions, such as liver or kidney disease, should avoid this type of diet.

The diet may cause some temporary side effects, such as headaches, weakness, fatigue, and constipation. It can also increase the risk of nutrient deficiencies, including vitamin and mineral deficiencies.

2. Is it a good way to lose weight? Or a terrible way to lose weight?

The keto diet can be effective for weight loss, as it causes the body to enter a state of ketosis, in which it burns fat for energy instead of glucose. However, it's not a sustainable long-term solution for weight loss, and the weight loss may not be sustainable or healthy in the long term.

Moreover, the long-term effects of the keto diet on weight maintenance and overall health are not yet fully understood. Therefore, it is important to consult with a qualified healthcare professional before starting the keto diet or any other diet for weight loss.

As a virtual assistant, I'm happy to provide you with some advice on how to support your friend. Panic attacks, fear, and anxiety can be overwhelming and can disrupt daily life. Here are a few tips that you can try:

1. Be an active listener: When your friend is experiencing anxiety or a panic attack, listen to them without judgment, and try to understand what they are going through. Let them know that you are there for them and that you care about them. Encourage them to express their feelings and thoughts.

2. Encourage your friend to seek professional help: Anxiety and panic attacks can be treated with therapy, medication, or a combination of both. Encourage your friend to seek professional help from a mental health professional. You can offer to accompany them to their first session or help them find a therapist.

3. Learn some coping techniques: There are several techniques that can help manage anxiety symptoms. These include deep breathing, mindfulness, progressive muscle relaxation, and visualization. Encourage your friend to try out some of these techniques.

4. Support healthy lifestyle choices: A healthy lifestyle, including regular exercise, a balanced diet, and enough sleep, can help reduce anxiety symptoms. Encourage your friend to engage in healthy activities and provide support when needed.

5. Respect your friend's boundaries: Anxiety can make it difficult to socialize or talk to others. If your friend needs space, respect their boundaries and let them know that they can reach out to you when they are ready.

It is not uncommon for people with anxiety to distance themselves from others, even close friends or family members. This behavior may be due to feelings of shame, guilt, or fear of being a burden. It is important to respect your friend's choice to take space, but also let them know that you are there for them when they need you.

input: Hello doctor,My son is 26 months old. The doctor has advised taking Menactra vaccine (Sanofi). Though this vaccine is not listed in IAP compulsory immunization schudule, is it necessary to take this? What are its side effects?
answer_icliniq: Hello. Menactra Vaccine (Meningococcal Vaccine (Group A, C, Y, and W-135)) is given to prevent meningococcal disease. It is not mentioned in IAP (Indian Academy of Pediatrics) compulsory immunization schedule. But in some places where meningococcus is prevalent, this vaccine is generally advised to be administered. This vaccine has some side effects like low fever, redness or pain at the site of vaccine injection and irritability. But these are very mild in nature and can occur with other vaccines also. In my opinion, it would be better if you give the vaccine to the child because meningococcal is a fatal disease and management is very difficult if the disease occurs in a child. For more information consult a child health specialist online
answer_chatgpt:
Menactra vaccine is a meningococcal conjugate vaccine that protects against four types of bacteria that can cause meningitis and bloodstream infections. While Menactra is not a part of the compulsory immunization schedule, it is recommended for certain individuals, including young children, teenagers, and adults who are at increased risk of contracting meningococcal disease, such as those with certain medical conditions and those traveling to areas with high rates of the disease.

As with any vaccine, Menactra may cause side effects, such as pain and swelling at the injection site, fever, headache, and muscle aches. These side effects are usually mild and go away on their own within a few days. Serious side effects from Menactra are rare but can include allergic reactions or fainting.

Overall, the benefits of Menactra vaccine in protecting against potentially life-threatening meningococcal disease outweigh the risks of side effects. It is important to talk to your child's doctor about Menactra vaccine and whether it is recommended for your child based on your child's individual circumstances and medical history.
answer_chatdoctor: 1) No, meningitis vaccines are NOT part of India compulsory childhood immunizations and would need to be taken under private prescription.2) Side effect profile of Menactra contains local pain and redness at injection site, fever (low grade), headache, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, swelling or tenderness at injection site, mild bruising, severe allergic reactions (difficulty breathing; hives; itching; rash; tightness in chest; unusual hoarseness); joint pain/swelling.3) The duration of protection is not known for meningococcal serogroup C.

input: Hello doctor,What is a concussion? What signs should I look for in a newborn to see if he has a concussion?
answer_icliniq: Hi. A concussion is an injury to the head and consequently brain, that is caused by a fall or a hit. The danger is baby might bleed inside the brain. Three danger signs to look for in a baby with a concussion are: The type of injury also matters. Sometimes even if no signs may be present, it is better that the baby is seen by a doctor. Usually, if the baby is fine for 48 hours after the injury, then it is unlikely that baby could have suffered a serious injury. CT scan of the brain is done to rule out any bleeds, in case of suspicion.
answer_chatgpt:
Hello! A concussion is a type of brain injury that occurs when the head is hit, jolted or shaken. It can cause a disruption in the normal functioning of the brain and can lead to a wide range of symptoms.

In newborns, signs of a concussion can be difficult to detect since they are not able to communicate their symptoms. However, if your newborn has suffered a blow to the head, here are some signs you should look out for:

1. Changes in feeding patterns, such as decreased appetite or difficulty latching onto a breast or bottle.
2. Changes in sleeping patterns, such as increased sleepiness or difficulty in waking up.
3. Irritability, excessive crying or fussiness.
4. Abnormal eye movement or lack of focusing on objects.
5. Persistent vomiting or spitting up after feeding.

If you notice any of these signs, it is important to seek medical attention immediately. A doctor will likely perform a physical exam and potentially imaging tests to determine if there is a concussion or any other serious injury.
answer_chatdoctor: A concussion is a traumatic brain injury that causes temporary alteration of consciousness. It can be caused by a fall, a blow to head, an automobile accident or any other mechanism causing sudden acceleration-deceleration of the head. Symptoms include dizziness, loss of balance, nausea and vomiting, blurred vision, slurred speech, headache, confusion, disorientation and memory problems. In newborns, it may present with seizures, excessive sleepiness, poor feeding, lethargy, irritability and poor muscle tone.

input: Hello,Welcome to ChatDoctor.Why a pimple should not be popped? When to pop a pimple? What to do after popping a pimple? What should I apply to heal the pimple and to avoid acne scars?

answer_chatgpt:
Hello! I'm glad you came to talk about your skin concerns. To answer your questions:

1. A pimple should not be popped because it can cause further inflammation, infection, and scarring. When a pimple is popped, the bacteria and oil from inside the pimple can spread to other areas of the skin, leading to more breakouts.

2. In general, it's best to avoid popping pimples altogether. However, if you have a particularly painful or noticeable pimple, and you feel that popping it will improve your overall skin appearance, there are some safe ways to do it. First, make sure the pimple has come to a head and is ready to be popped. Wash your hands and the affected area with warm water and a gentle cleanser. Use a sterile needle or a comedone extractor to gently remove the pus or whitehead. Avoid squeezing or pressing too hard, as this can cause damage to the skin. Afterward, cleanse the area with a mild toner, and apply an acne spot treatment.

3. After popping a pimple, it's important to keep the area clean and moisturized. You should avoid touching the area as much as possible to prevent further irritation. Apply an ice pack or a cold, damp cloth to reduce swelling and inflammation. You may also want to apply a topical antibiotic cream or ointment to prevent infection.

4. To heal the pimple and avoid acne scars, try using products that contain salicylic acid, benzoyl peroxide, or tea tree oil. These ingredients can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that might be causing your breakouts. You can also use a spot treatment that contains hydrocolloid bandages, which can help speed up the healing process and prevent scarring.

It's important to note that everyone's skin is different, and what works for one person may not work for another. If you're having persistent acne or scarring, it's important to talk to a dermatologist or skincare professional for personalized advice and treatment options.

Feeling nauseous after a workout can happen for many reasons. Intense exercise can cause your blood vessels to expand, your blood pressure to drop, and your stomach to feel queasy. Additionally, dehydration, low blood sugar, and even anxiety can lead to feelings of nausea after working out.

If you regularly feel nauseous after working out, it's important to speak with a healthcare professional to determine the underlying cause.

To prevent nausea before a workout, you may want to avoid eating large meals or foods that are high in fat or fiber, which can slow down digestion. Instead, opt for a light snack high in carbohydrates and low in fat before your workout, such as a banana or a piece of toast with honey.

During your workout, it's important to stay hydrated by drinking water or a sports drink. It might also be helpful to take frequent breaks to let your body rest and recover.

After your workout, cool down with gentle stretches to help your body gradually return to its resting state. You can also refuel with a snack or meal that is high in carbohydrates and protein, such as a smoothie with Greek yogurt and berries.

High-intensity workouts, such as HIIT (high-intensity interval training), are more likely to cause nausea because they require a lot of physical exertion. However, this doesn't mean you should avoid them altogether. If you experience nausea during a workout, take a break, rest, and wait for the symptoms to pass.

Finally, foods that are high in electrolytes, such as bananas or coconut water, can help prevent nausea by replenishing lost fluids and minerals during your workout.

input: Dear doctor,I am taking SNRI/ SSRI for around 20 years. Five years ago, my general anxiety got worse. So, my doctor switched from Citalopram 10 mg to Escitalopram 10 mg (one per day). Citalopram was taken five years to 10 years ago, before that Zoloft 20 mg per day. Now my GAD is getting worse. A friend of mine had a good experience with Buspirone. Could you explain me the general difference between Escitalopram and Buspirone?
answer_icliniq: Hello, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. Buspirone is a different  ChatDoctor.  Escitalopram is an SSRI (selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor) and helps improve serotonergic neurotransmission in the brain. The
answer_chatgpt:
Dear patient,

Escitalopram and Buspirone are very different types of medications that are used to treat anxiety disorders.

Escitalopram belongs to a class of medications called selective serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs). It works by increasing the levels of the neurotransmitter serotonin in the brain, which helps to improve mood and reduce anxiety. Escitalopram is commonly used to treat generalized anxiety disorder (GAD), as well as major depression and panic disorder.

Buspirone, on the other hand, belongs to a class of medications called anxiolytics. It works by binding to serotonin and dopamine receptors in the brain, which helps to reduce anxiety and improve mood. Buspirone is not a benzodiazepine and does not have the same potential for abuse or dependence as benzodiazepine medications, which are commonly used to treat anxiety disorders.

The main difference between these medications is how they work in the brain. Escitalopram primarily affects the levels of serotonin in the brain, while Buspirone affects both serotonin and dopamine receptors. Buspirone is usually considered a second-line treatment for anxiety disorders, while SSRIs like escitalopram are considered first-line treatments.

It is important to note that everyone responds differently to medications and there are many factors that can influence which medication is best for an individual. Therefore, it is important to talk to your doctor about your symptoms and any concerns you may have, and together you can decide which medication is best for you.
answer_chatdoctor: Hi, I have read your question and understand your concerns. The main difference between Escitalopram and Buspirone is their mechanism of action. Escitalopram is an antidepressant belonging to the selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor group (SSRI) while Buspirone is an anti-anxiety medication belonging to the piperazine group. In other words Escitalopram increases the levels of Serotonin in the brain while Buspirone enhances its effect by increasing the sensitivity of the Serotonin receptors in the brain. Both treat anxiety but Escitalopram is more effective in treating depression as well. Hope this answers your question. Please feel free to ask if you have further queries.

input: Hi doctor,I heard that in nephrotic syndrome the filtering unit of kidney becomes larger and so protein comes in urine. I am a patient of cortisone dependent nephrotic syndrome over 15 years. Doctor says that about 95 percent of children will get cured at the age of 18. I am 17 years old now. When a child grows, does the filtering unit become smaller? And what happens to the remaining 5 percent? I have anemia, but otherwise normal. Only albumin comes in urine. I also heard that kidney biopsy is done for nephrotic syndrome. But no doctor advised me for biopsy. Do I have minimal change disease?
answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. Yes, you have heard the things right. Due to increased pore there is protein in urine. The best treatment remains the Cortisone. In cases where there is no response to Cortisone as evidenced by persistence of albumin in urine, we need to shift those patients on Cyclophosphamide and Azathioprine. Renal biopsy can be performed but since you are on steroids the reports may be altered. You need to get C3 (complement component 3), C4 (complement component 4), ANA (antinuclear antibody), ANCA (antineutrophil cytoplasmic antibody) and urine protein to creatinine ratio.
answer_chatgpt:
Hello,

In nephrotic syndrome, the filtering unit of the kidney (glomerulus) becomes leaky, resulting in the loss of albumin and other proteins in the urine. This can lead to a decrease in the amount of protein in the body, including the protein needed to maintain the fluid balance in the bloodstream. 

In some cases, as children grow and their kidneys develop, the filtering unit of the kidney becomes less leaky, and the nephrotic syndrome may resolve. However, this is not always the case, and many individuals continue to experience nephrotic syndrome into adulthood.

The remaining 5 percent of children who do not "outgrow" their nephrotic syndrome by the age of 18 may have a primary kidney disease that requires more aggressive treatment.

In terms of your specific case, it is difficult to say whether you have minimal change disease without a kidney biopsy. Minimal change disease is a common cause of nephrotic syndrome in children, but there are other causes as well. A kidney biopsy is typically recommended to confirm the diagnosis and guide appropriate treatment, but it is possible that your doctor has determined that your symptoms and response to treatment do not warrant a biopsy at this time.

Anemia is a common complication of chronic kidney disease, including nephrotic syndrome. It is important to continue to work closely with your doctor to manage your symptoms and monitor for any changes in your kidney function or other markers of disease progression.

I hope this information is helpful. Please speak with your doctor if you have any further questions or concerns.
answer_chatdoctor: 1) Minimal Change Disease (MCD) is one type of glomerular diseases which can cause Nephrotic Syndrome.2) MCD usually occurs in child Chat Doctor. 3) It is characterized by decreased amounts of albumin in urine (albuminuria).4) The main treatment of choice is steroids and most patients respond well in steroid doses.5) However, some patients may require maintenance immunosuppressive therapy with cyclosporine or tacrolimus.6) A kidney biopsy is essential to make the diagnosis of MCD.

input: Hi doctor,I had unprotected sex with my girlfriend and she took I-pill within two hours. Is this a pregnancy or a disease amenorrhea? If amenorrhea happens, then can it be easily cured or not? How she can have her regular periods? answer_icliniq: Hi. Amenorrhea is not a disease. Amenorrhea is simply absence of the menstrual cycle. A lot of females have missed periods for one or two cycles after taking I-pill and this is normal. In most cases cycle resumes after one or two missed periods. So, no need to worry. Within one to two months her periods will resume. So, no need to do anything. As she bled three to six days after taking I-pill, there is no risk of pregnancy. The amenorrhea is the side effect of I-pill only. It is not a matter of fear. No need to do anything. Just remain relaxed and wait for her periods to resume.
answer_chatgpt:
Hello,

I- pill can cause changes in the menstrual cycle, including delayed or missed periods. Usually, this is a temporary effect and periods will return to their regular schedule in the next menstrual cycle.

However, in rare cases, amenorrhea (absence of periods) may occur as a side effect of I-pill, which may last for several months. If your girlfriend is experiencing amenorrhea, it is best to see a doctor for further evaluation.

Amenorrhea can also be caused by other factors like stress, weight gain or loss, certain medical conditions, hormonal imbalances, etc. Treatment will depend on the underlying cause of amenorrhea.

If pregnancy is suspected, a pregnancy test can be done to confirm it. If the test is negative, then other causes will be explored.

Overall, amenorrhea can be a serious concern if it persists for a longer time or if it is accompanied by other symptoms. So, it is best to see a doctor to diagnose the underlying cause and to get proper treatment.

input: Hi doctor,I have a boil on my inner thigh, which keeps recurring. I have had a boil in the same spot before. When I popped it went away, but now it has come back. It is also very painful. Please help.
answer_icliniq: Hi, Welcome to Chat Doctor forum. I have read your query. It seems that you are having furunculosis. Boils (also called furuncles) are a deep bacterial infection of hair follicles. The cause of recurrence may be Staphylococcus aureus, which is most commonly carried in the nostrils, armpits, between the legs and in the cleft between the buttocks. It may be transferred to other sites from the nostrils through the finger nails. General measures to avoid the recurrence are as follows. Regarding treatment, I suggest oral antibiotics like tablet Augmentin 625 mg (combination of Amoxicillin and Clavulanate potassium) if you are not allergic to penicillin group. Take one tablet thrice daily for five days. Also, my suggestion is to apply Mupirocin twice daily for five days. Consult your doctor, discuss with him or her and take the medicine with consent.

Hello! It sounds like you have a recurrent boil, which can be frustrating and painful.

Here are some things you can do to manage the boil:

1. Keep the area clean and dry. Wash the area with soap and warm water twice a day, and pat it dry with a clean towel.

2. Apply a warm compress to the boil. This can help bring the pus to the surface and allow it to drain. You can use a warm, damp towel or a heating pad set on low.

3. Avoid popping or squeezing the boil. This can make it worse and cause it to spread.

4. If the boil is particularly painful, you can take over-the-counter pain medication like ibuprofen or acetaminophen to help manage the discomfort.

5. If the boil doesn't go away or becomes larger, seek medical attention. Your doctor may need to drain the boil or prescribe antibiotics to treat any infection.

6. To prevent future recurrence, practice good hygiene by washing your hands frequently and avoid sharing personal items such as towels or razors with others.

I hope this helps! Don't hesitate to seek medical attention if needed.

The bloating, loose stools, burning sensation in the abdomen, and intolerance to certain foods are typical symptoms of functional dyspepsia. The hand and feet pains, headaches, and breathlessness may be related to anxiety or IBS.

The medication you are taking, Rabeprazole, is a proton pump inhibitor (PPI) that reduces stomach acid production and can improve symptoms of functional dyspepsia. However, long-term use of PPIs can cause side effects such as magnesium deficiency, bone loss, and an increased risk of infections.

For your symptoms, I would recommend a combination of lifestyle changes and medication. Lifestyle changes include avoiding trigger foods and eating smaller, more frequent meals, avoiding alcohol and smoking, reducing stress, and getting regular physical activity. Medications that can help with IBS symptoms include antispasmodics, fiber supplements, and antidepressants.

I would also recommend discussing the possibility of tapering off the PPI with your doctor and monitoring your magnesium levels. If your symptoms persist or worsen, you may benefit from further testing or referral to a gastroenterologist.

Overall, it is important to work closely with your healthcare provider to manage your symptoms and find a treatment plan that works best for you.

input: Hello doctor,I am 26 years old and have been a type-1 diabetic for 24 years. I am considering getting pregnant. Is there a specific A1C number I should aim for before trying to get pregnant? Aside from having a great A1C and taking prenatal vitamins, is there anything else I should be doing to prepare for diabetic pregnancy? Also, is there a chance of delivering vaginally? I have heard that diabetics almost always have to have c-sections, but I would like to avoid it if possible.
answer_icliniq: Hello. A diabetic patient like you should have preconception counseling regarding your sugar levels, HbA1c as for conception you need to have your HbA1c below 48 mmol/mol or 6.1 %. Even HbA1C level above 86 mmol/mol or 10 % is strongly against pregnancy. So, try to avoid it if you have this level as more chances of miscarriage, fetal congenital anomalies, etc. A diabetic patient having BMI greater than 27 kg/m2 and want to conceive is advised to reduce weight before trying for conception. For more information consult an obstetrician and gynecologist online  Take care.
answer_chatgpt:
Hello! That's great news that you are considering pregnancy. Managing blood sugar levels is critical during pregnancy to minimize risks to both the mother and the developing baby. The target A1C level for women with type-1 diabetes who are considering pregnancy is typically below 6.5%, as higher levels increase the risk of complications. However, the target may vary based on an individual's health history and other risk factors.

Besides managing blood sugar levels and taking prenatal vitamins, it is recommended to start planning a few months before conceiving by:

- Consulting with an endocrinologist who has expertise in diabetic pregnancies and work together to achieve stable blood sugar control.
- Monitoring blood sugar levels more closely in the months leading up to pregnancy.
- Developing a pregnancy plan that includes regular prenatal checkups and appointments with an obstetrician and endocrinologist.
- Discussing any complications or risk factors that may increase the risk of gestational diabetes, preeclampsia, or other medical conditions during pregnancy.

Regarding the delivery mode, every woman's situation is unique, and the decision will be made by a combination of factors like the size and position of the baby, the mother's health, fetal well-being, and medical history. Many diabetic women can have a vaginal delivery; however, some may have to undergo a c-section. It is important to discuss this with your obstetrician, and make a plan that is safe for you and your baby.
answer_chatdoctor: 1) You need to keep your blood sugar levels well under control to prevent problems in pregnancy. 2) Aim for an HbA1c level less than 7% (or less than 53 mmol/mol).3) Take folic acid supplements as soon as you start trying to conceive, even if you are not sure when you will become pregnant.4) Do regular physical activity.5) Eat healthy foods including fruits and vegetables every day.6) Monitor your blood pressure and weight regularly.

input: Hello doctor,1. Why we are getting frequent colds?2. What can be done to reduce the amount of colds we are getting?

answer_chatgpt:
Hello! As an AI language model, I am not a doctor and I cannot interpret your specific symptoms. However, I can provide general information about the common cold and tips to reduce your risk of getting one.

1. Why we are getting frequent colds?

The common cold is caused by a viral infection in the upper respiratory tract. There are many different types of cold viruses, so it's possible to catch a cold multiple times in a year. In addition, some people may be more susceptible to colds due to a weaker immune system or exposure to cold and flu viruses in their environment.

2. What can be done to reduce the amount of colds we are getting?

There are several things you can do to reduce your risk of catching a cold:

- Wash your hands regularly with soap and water, especially before eating or after being in contact with someone who is sick.
- Avoid touching your face, especially your eyes, nose, and mouth, as this can transfer cold viruses from your hands to your respiratory system.
- Stay away from people who are sick, and avoid crowded areas if possible.
- Boost your immune system by eating a healthy diet, getting enough sleep, and exercising regularly.
- Consider getting an annual flu shot, which can help protect against some strains of the flu virus that can also cause cold-like symptoms.

If you do catch a cold, it's important to rest, drink plenty of fluids, and avoid spreading the virus to others by covering your mouth and nose when you cough or sneeze.

input: Hello doctor,I have a question about total protein count in blood, urine, and saliva. My question is, does someone\s total protein count change significantly based on how much protein rich food they eat? Or does someone\s total protein count not change unless they have an illness like muscle damage? When I say total protein count I am referring to what is calculated in a biuret test.
answer_icliniq: Hi. The protein is digested in our human body but is sometimes excreted when not digested properly or in excess quantity. The term which you are referring to, which is total protein count in blood, urine and saliva has totally different meaning in all the three mediums. Total protein count in blood changes with increased protein ingestion and already present muscle protein or any other protein breakdown inside the body whereas protein in urine or any excretory product is always when the degradation of protein has increased, which can be possible because of higher intake of protein or increased muscle breakdown due to some pathology inside the body. I hope that I have answered your question. Let me know if you have any other doubts.
answer_chatgpt:
Hello! The total protein count in blood, urine, and saliva can vary depending on several factors, including diet, hydration status, and underlying medical conditions. 

If someone eats a protein-rich diet, their total protein count may increase temporarily due to the excess of protein in their system. However, this increase is not significant and normally does not cause any concern. If someone consistently eats a high protein diet, their total protein count may increase to some degree, but it is unlikely to cause significant changes in their biuret test results.

However, in certain medical conditions, such as kidney disease or liver disease, the total protein count may significantly rise or decrease, indicating a problem with protein metabolism or filtration. Other conditions, such as infections or inflammation, can also cause changes in the total protein count.

Muscle damage can also affect the total protein count as it releases proteins into the bloodstream, including creatinine kinase and myoglobin. However, this is unlikely to affect the biuret test results directly.

It is important to note that the interpretation of the total protein count in blood, urine, or saliva relies on clinical context, and other lab results and clinical information should also be taken into consideration.
answer_chatdoctor: 1) Yes, your total protein level can increase with increased intake of protein-rich food.2) Your total protein level is usually slightly higher than normal in healthy individuals, so it may vary with changes in dietary habits.3) The total protein level in urine and saliva is usually low compared to that in blood, but it can be elevated in certain conditions (like kidney disease).4) The biuret test measures the amount of nitrogen in your serum, which is mostly due to proteins.

Fordyce spots are a common and harmless condition that can affect both men and women. They are small, raised, and white or yellowish bumps that may appear around the mouth or on the genitals.

The spots occur when sebaceous glands, which are the glands responsible for producing oil in the skin, become clogged with oil or other debris. This causes the glands to become enlarged and visible as raised bumps on the skin.

Unfortunately, there is no cure for Fordyce spots. However, there are some treatments that can help reduce their appearance or make them less noticeable. These include:

- Topical creams or ointments containing tretinoin or alpha hydroxy acids, which can help exfoliate and unclog the pores
- Laser treatments, which can target and remove the spots
- Electrodessication, which involves using an electric needle to destroy the affected glands

As for the tight hard masses that you feel when you touch the spots on your penis, it is possible that these are enlarged sebaceous glands or cysts. These are also benign conditions and usually do not require treatment unless they become painful or infected.

I would recommend consulting with a dermatologist or urologist who can examine you and provide a proper diagnosis and treatment options. They may also want to perform some tests to rule out any underlying medical conditions.

Take care.

input: Hi doctor,I got my AC joint separated while mountain biking. It is grade 5. I am getting surgery and I want to know whether I should use an autograft (hamstring) or allograft from a cadaver. I am very active in outdoor adventures and sports. Thanks for your help.
answer_icliniq: Hi. I am sorry to hear about your injury. The good news is that there are many modalities available for AC joint injuries (acromioclavicular joint), which are showing promising results.  Take care.
answer_chatgpt:
Hello! I'm sorry to hear about your injury, but glad to hear that you will be getting surgery to treat your AC joint separation. 

Regarding the choice between autograft and allograft, there are pros and cons to both options. 

Autografts are taken from your own body, usually from the hamstring or patellar tendon. The benefit is that there is no risk of disease transmission, and the chance of rejection is greatly reduced. Autografts also offer better incorporation, and are typically stronger and more durable than allografts. However, a disadvantage of autografts is the possibility of donor site morbidity, such as discomfort or weakness in the area where the graft was taken from. 

Allografts, on the other hand, are taken from a cadaver and can be used for a variety of purposes, including AC joint reconstruction. Allografts eliminate the need to harvest tissue from the patient, and can provide a larger graft than autograft options. However, there is a slightly higher risk of disease transmission with allografts compared to autografts. 

Ultimately, the decision on which graft to use will be up to you and your surgeon. Factors to consider may include the specifics of your injury, your age, and your overall health. It's important to have an open and frank discussion with your surgeon about the pros and cons of each option and the risks and benefits of each.

I hope this information is helpful!
answer_chatdoctor: 1. The choice of graft depends on availability, cost, donor site morbidity etc.2. Hamstrings are generally preferred because they have maximum vascularization, good blood supply, lesser risk of infection, and more strength after healing.3. Autograft has its own advantages like no chance of rejection and immune response but it is not as strong as hamstrings.4. Allograft is rarely used nowadays due to its disadvantages. Hope I could answer your query.

input: Hello doctor,I was recommended by my cardiologist for a heart valve replacement surgery as my latest echocardiogram results, both transthoracic (TTE) and transesophageal (TEE), revealed that my mitral valve prolapse had worsened. The diagnosis was severe eccentric MR. However, during the latest TTE scan, the radiographer who did the scan said that my regurgitation was only moderate. I find this very confusing and was wondering if MVP is a reversible condition. No current medications.
answer_icliniq: Hello. First of all, the severity of MR (mitral regurgitation) will be correctly deciphered by your cardiologist. An eccentric type of MR is actually a grade higher than what it looks. Like your radiologist may think it is moderate but it is severe, but because of its eccentric nature, it looks lesser than what it is. The second thing is, why are you not on any medicine yet? You should be on ACE (angiotensin-converting enzyme) inhibitors and diuretics. If you have symptoms like breathlessness, these medicines will relieve that and prevent heart events too. If you have symptoms and your heart chamber size increases, then you should go for MVR (mitral valve replacement). If a prolapse is worsening, then it will not reverse. For more information consult a cardiologist online  Take care.
answer_chatgpt:
Hello! It is not uncommon for there to be some discrepancy between the severity of mitral valve regurgitation detected by TTE and TEE. TEE is generally considered to be the gold standard imaging modality for evaluating mitral valve regurgitation. Nonetheless, the decision to proceed with a mitral valve surgery is based on several factors, including the severity of the regurgitation, the presence of symptoms, and the overall health and age of the patient. 

In general, mitral valve prolapse (MVP) is not a reversible condition, but the severity of the regurgitation can be managed with medications, and in some cases, treated with surgery. Medications such as beta-blockers and ACE inhibitors can help with symptoms such as palpitations, shortness of breath, or chest discomfort. Additionally, surgery, either to repair or replace the mitral valve, can be recommended for patients with severe regurgitation and/or symptoms.

It is important to have an open and honest discussion with your cardiologist, who can provide you with more detailed information on the severity of your condition and the recommended treatment options specific to your case.

A mild grade leakage is usually associated with no symptoms at all while a more serious degree can cause breathlessness, palpitations, chest pain etc. It is best assessed by an experienced Cardiac Surgeon after a detailed examination of your heart.

input: Hello doctor,I was involved in a car accident which my vehicle ran reverse on my left arm and got swelling and bruises all over my arm and now when I try to lay my arm down on something, there is a lot of pain in the elbow area and around the top of the elbow area and the doctor says there is nothing broken. I am just wondering what I should do. It is warm to the touch.
answer_icliniq: Hello. There must be crushing of the tissues and what is called internal degloving and a collection of blood called a hematoma and that causes the part to be swollen and warm to touch. As long as there is no fracture then you do not need a plaster but a sling to rest the arm is mandatory till the pain comes under control and the swelling reduces.  The sling will also enforce rest to the part and will help keep the forearm from swelling up. Ice the arm and elbow 20 minutes for five to six times a day. Elevate when in bed on two pillows. If no better in a week then re-visit a doctor for a review.
answer_chatgpt:
Based on your description of the injury, it sounds like you may have a soft tissue injury such as a sprain or strain. It's good to hear that your doctor confirmed there are no fractures, but you should continue to follow their advice on how to manage the injury.

To help reduce swelling, you can apply a cold compress to the affected area for 15-20 minutes at a time, several times a day. Avoid putting any pressure on the injured arm and keep it elevated as much as possible.

You can also take over-the-counter pain medication such as ibuprofen or acetaminophen to help manage your pain. Be sure to follow the dosage instructions on the label and consult your doctor before taking any new medication.

If your symptoms persist or worsen, be sure to follow up with your doctor. They may suggest additional treatment options such as physical therapy or a referral to a specialist for further evaluation.
